title: Reno Direct & Service Requests Report – March 2026
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: April 21, 2026
TO: Honorable Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Cynthia Esparza, Director
Noemi Gomez Martinez, Community Relationships Manager
DEPT: Community Engagement and Services
SUBJECT: Reno Direct & Service Requests Report – March 2026

Monthly reports provide insights into how constituents engage with the City through Reno Direct, the City's centralized customer service center and ServiceNow platform.

Key report highlights include:
• Number of Reno Direct constituent interactions (calls, voicemails, emails, online chats)
• Number of service requests submitted citywide
• Number of service requests closed citywide
• Top five trending service request types citywide
• Top five trending service request types by ward

This information supports Community Engagement and Services efforts to enhance service delivery while addressing community needs in a responsive and equitable manner.

Attachments:
• March 2026 Service Request Trends & Interaction Report

fields.authors: Cynthia Esparza, Director; Noemi Gomez Martinez, Community Relationships Manager
fields.tldr: The March 2026 Reno Direct report summarizes constituent interactions and service request activity across all city departments. The centralized customer service center handled 2,819 total interactions and received 2,978 new service requests citywide, while closing 3,030 requests. Illegal/abandoned parking was the top service request type both citywide and in every individual ward.
fields.key_stats: Total constituent interactions: 2,819 (1,963 calls answered, 401 voicemails, 439 emails, 16 online chats); New service requests: 2,978; Closed service requests: 3,030; Top request citywide: Parking Illegally/Abandoned (951); Largest department by new requests: Reno Police & Parking Enforcement (1,376 new, 1,472 closed).

people: Noemi Gomez Martinez, Community Relationships Manager
